query:
UPDATE lessons SET content = '## Lesson Overview

The Book of Revelation, written by the apostle John during exile on Patmos, is the New Testament''s only apocalyptic book. It reveals Jesus Christ in His glorified state, addresses seven churches in Asia Minor, and presents visions of future events culminating in Christ''s return, final judgment, and the new creation. Revelation provides hope for persecuted believers and assurance of God''s ultimate victory.

## Learning Objectives

Upon completing this lesson, students will be able to:
- Identify the major sections and themes of Revelation
- Understand the various interpretive approaches to Revelation
- Explain the significance of the letters to the seven churches
- Describe the ultimate hope presented in Revelation 21-22

## Structure of Revelation

Revelation follows a clear outline based on John''s commission in 1:19. Chapter 1 presents the glorified Christ. Chapters 2-3 contain letters to seven churches. Chapters 4-5 depict heavenly worship and the Lamb''s worthiness. Chapters 6-18 describe judgments through seals, trumpets, and bowls. Chapter 19 presents Christ''s return. Chapter 20 addresses the millennium and final judgment. Chapters 21-22 reveal the new heaven and new earth.

## The Seven Churches

Christ''s letters to the seven churches of Asia Minor address specific conditions in each congregation while providing timeless principles for all churches. Each letter follows a similar pattern: identification of Christ, commendation (except Laodicea), criticism (except Smyrna and Philadelphia), exhortation, and promise to overcomers.

## Interpretive Approaches

Four major approaches interpret Revelation: preterist (fulfilled in the first century), historicist (fulfilled throughout church history), futurist (largely future fulfillment), and idealist (symbolic of the ongoing conflict between good and evil). Each approach offers insights while facing challenges.

## The Blessed Hope

Revelation culminates in the glorious vision of the new Jerusalem, where God dwells with His people, death and sorrow are abolished, and believers enjoy eternal fellowship with God and the Lamb. This hope sustains believers through present trials.

## Key Scripture References

- Revelation 1:7-8 - Christ''s coming and deity
- Revelation 5:9-10 - The Lamb''s worthiness
- Revelation 19:11-16 - Christ''s triumphant return
- Revelation 21:1-4 - The new heaven and earth

## Summary

Revelation assures believers that despite present persecution and apparent evil triumph, God remains sovereign and will ultimately vindicate His people. Christ will return in glory, judge the wicked, and establish His eternal kingdom. The book calls believers to faithful endurance and provides hope for the future.' WHERE id = 720004;
command:
mysql --batch --raw --column-names --default-character-set=utf8mb4 --host gateway02.us-east-1.prod.aws.tidbcloud.com --port 4000 --user 2KiCrCqNDFcXt7P.577d1a63eeb5 --database P2Dpw9MQsmxzdUQCfGjV6b --execute UPDATE lessons SET content = '## Lesson Overview

- Revelation 19:11-16 - Christ''s triumphant return

Christ will return in glory, judge the wicked, and establish His eternal kingdom. The book calls believers to faithful endurance and provides hope for the future.' WHERE id = 720004;
